message:
feat(io): add StreamAccounting for BrowserReadable/WritableStream

Introduces StreamAccounting, a RAII bookkeeper that tracks bytes
read/written and terminal state (Closed vs Aborted) against an
optional Arc<StreamStats>. Key properties:

- Exactly-once terminal counting: mark_closed/mark_aborted are
  idempotent after the first terminal transition
- Drop safety: if dropped while still Open, counts as aborted
  (no leaked streams in stats)
- Zero-cost when stats are None (all branches short-circuit)

Both BrowserReadableStream and BrowserWritableStream now carry a
StreamAccounting field, with accounting calls wired into every
read/write/close/error path. A with_stats() constructor enables
stats collection; the public new() constructor passes None.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
